channel.title: Daily Feed (Updated 9 AM BD)
channel.description: Daily Feed (Updated 9 AM BD) generated by script

item.title: US African Development Foundation wins case against Trump admin
item.description: A federal judge has removed Peter Marocco from USADF, a ruling that comes after a yearlong legal battle, mass terminations, and a corruption scandal involving a senior USADF official.

item.title: Iran Conflict: “Civil War Will Be Inevitable”
item.description: Iranian and Israeli flags fill the centre of Manchester, in northern England. There are also portraits of a king overthrown almost half a century ago and of his son, now a claimant to the throne from exile. It is yet another march of Iranians calling for Reza Pahlavi as an alternative to the regime of […]

item.title: The aid collapse is a stress test for locally built health tech
item.description: Opinion: When USAID disappears overnight, countries that built grant-dependent digital health systems break. Countries that built their own don't.

item.title: KOFI TIME – The Podcast
item.description: About Kofi Time – The Podcast Join a journey of discovery as Ahmad Fawzi interviews some of Kofi Annan’s closest advisors and colleagues, including Dr Peter Piot, Christiane Amanpour, Mark Malloch-Brown, Michael Møller, Mark Suzman, Alicia Bárcena and more. In each episode, Ahmad Fawzi, a former spokesperson and Communication Advisor to Kofi Annan, […]

item.title: Donor seat on education SDG committee vacant amid funding crisis
item.description: A key donor seat on the global body for SDG 4 is still empty — just as education aid is set to hit record lows.

item.title: Ranking U.S. Presidents: Best and Worst
item.description: Throughout its 250-year history, following the Declaration of Independence on July 4, 1776, the United States has elected 47 presidents. From George Washington in 1789 to Donald Trump in 2024, each U.S. president has left their mark on the nation and the world in various ways. Some presidents are celebrated for their foresight, character, and […]

item.title: Devex Invested: A tale of two development banks
item.description: From Paraguay to Luxembourg, IDB and EIB sketch out two visions for development finance.

item.title: Kenya’s flood response shows the promise — and limits — of ‘One Health’
item.description: When floods in Kenya’s Tana River County sparked a cholera outbreak in 2024, officials responded using the “One Health” approach. The response worked. But funding gaps threaten its future.

item.title: The new reality for global development consultants
item.description: After 2025’s aid cuts, consultants face a reshaped market. Devex explores what this means for careers in the sector.

item.title: Philippines: ICC Hearing Gives Survivors of Duterte’s Drug War Hope
item.description: Gito* had just arrived at his father’s house in Caloocan City in the Philippines on December 7, 2016, when three armed policemen burst into the home, grabbed his father, took him outside and shot him multiple times. Gito told IPS his father had put his hands up when the officers told him they had come […]

item.title: MIDDLE EAST LIVE 17 March: Crisis impact ripples across region
item.description: The crisis in the Middle East continues, with strikes and counterstrikes reported across the region as the humanitarian toll rises. UN agencies warn that health systems are under growing strain, children are increasingly affected by violence and displacement, and hundreds of thousands have been forced from their homes - especially in Lebanon. Despite mounting logistical and security challenges, UN agencies are scaling up emergency aid. Stay with us for live updates from across the UN system. App users can follow coverage here.

item.title: Afghanistan: UN condemns deadly attack on rehab centre in Kabul
item.description: Top UN officials condemned on Tuesday Pakistan’s overnight strike on a rehab centre that reportedly killed at least 400 people in Kabul and injured more than 250 others.

item.title: Middle East war risks pushing 45 million more people into acute hunger
item.description: The Middle East war could cause the worst disruption to lifesaving humanitarian work since COVID, the UN World Food Programme (WFP) warned on Tuesday.

item.title: World News in Brief: West Bank displacement, Cuba fuel crisis, Sexual abuse safeguards, New ‘humancentric’ AI Advocate
item.description: A new UN human rights report warns that expanding Israeli settlements are driving large-scale displacement of Palestinians across the occupied West Bank.

item.title: ‘No matter what’, UN Women vows to stay in Afghanistan, amid deepening rights crisis
item.description: UN Women will continue delivering for Afghan women and girls despite sweeping restrictions and ongoing instability, a senior official said on Tuesday.

item.title: Devex Newswire: US Congress defies Trump with $600M for family planning
item.description: What becomes of the $600 million allocated by Congress for reproductive health and family planning? Plus, the state of investment in gender-driven development.

item.title: Rapid Rise of Smart City Surveillance Tech Across Africa to Spy on Citizens
item.description: A massive expansion of AI-enabled surveillance of public spaces across Africa is violating citizens’ freedoms and the fundamental human right to privacy, warns a new report by the Institute of Development Studies. African governments are paying billions of dollars to Chinese companies for so-called ‘smart city’ products for public space surveillance – including AI-enabled CCTV […]

item.title: At CSW70, Advocates Warn Conflict Is Deepening Barriers to Justice for Women and Girls
item.description: The 70th session of the Commission on the Status of Women (CSW70) has brought together global leaders, gender equity advocates, and youth representatives at the United Nations (UN) Headquarters to advance efforts to strengthen mechanisms for justice, equality, and representation for women and girls worldwide. With challenges particularly pronounced in conflict zones, this year’s priority […]
